paper_title: Analysis of the grass pollen season in selected Polish cities in 2018

methods.description: The process of measuring pollen concentration in study sites using volumetric methods with Burkard and Lanzoni pollen samplers.

steps.description: The measurements of the pollen concentration in the study sites were performed with the volumetric method using Burkard and Lanzoni pollen samplers.
steps.searchable_summary: use Burkard and Lanzoni pollen samplers to measure pollen concentration

methods.method_name: Microscopic Observation and Pollen Count Analysis
methods.description: Microscopic observations and analysis of pollen counts over a 7-day cycle to determine daily pollen concentration and seasonal pollen index.

steps.description: Microscopic observations were performed on preparations obtained in a 7-day cycle with assessment of 24-hour periods.
steps.searchable_summary: perform microscopic observations on 7-day cycle preparations

steps.description: The results were expressed as the number of pollen grains in 1 m3 of air per day (P/m3).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate daily pollen grains per cubic meter

steps.description: The start of the season was defined as a date when 1% of the seasonal cumulative pollen count was trapped and the end of the season when cumulative pollen count reached 99%.
steps.searchable_summary: determine start and end of pollen season based on cumulative count

steps.description: The total pollen count over this period was expressed by the symbol SPI (Seasonal Pollen Index).
The course of the pollen seasons in each city is shown in the graphs (figs [1][2][3][4][5][6]).
steps.searchable_summary: create graphs to show pollen season course in each city
